data.title: The Cyberspace Administration of China plans to strengthen the management of digital virtual human information services.
data.description: The Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) has released a notice soliciting public opinions on the "Administrative Measures for Digital Virtual Human Information Services (Draft for Comment)," which aims to strengthen the management of digital virtual human information services. The draft stipulates that from the commencement of digital virtual human services, service providers, users, and providers of online information content dissemination services must continuously display a prominent sign containing the word "digital human" in the digital virtual human display area throughout the entire process, and comply with relevant national regulations on the labeling of artificial intelligence-generated and synthesized content. (Xinhua News Agency)

data.title: China Proposes New Regulations for Digital Virtual Human Services
data.description: China's Cyberspace Administration has released a draft proposal aimed at enhancing the management of digital virtual human information services. According to Foresight News, the proposal outlines requirements for service providers and users to display a prominent label indicating 'digital human' throughout the duration of the service. This measure is intended to comply with national regulations concerning the labeling of AI-generated synthetic content.

data.title: Glassnode: BTC whales and sharks averaged a daily loss of approximately $337 million in Q1, with total losses exceeding $30.9 billion for the year.
data.description: On-chain data from GlassNode shows that in Q1 2026, "sharks" holding 100-1,000 Bitcoins and "whales" holding 1,000-10,000 Bitcoins incurred average daily losses of approximately $188.5 million and $147.5 million respectively, totaling approximately $337 million. The cumulative locked-in losses for the year reached a staggering $30.9 billion, approaching the levels seen during the 2022 bear market. Analysis indicates that current selling pressure stems from rising macroeconomic risks (inflation expectations, AI trading congestion, etc.) and weakening market confidence, with large holders accelerating stop-loss exits. Meanwhile, long-term holders (LTH) continue to incur average daily losses of approximately $200 million, indicating that the market has not yet shown significant "selling pressure exhaustion." Institutions believe that under multiple pressures, Bitcoin still faces further downside risks, with some predicting a potential bottom range of $40,000 to $50,000. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitcoin treasury firm Hyperscale Data received $26.6 million in litigation settlement, increasing its holdings to approximately 633 Bitcoins.
data.description: Hyperscale Data, a US-listed Bitcoin treasury company, announced that its subsidiaries Ault Lending LLC and RiskOn International have received approximately $26.6 million in settlement payments from a multi-year litigation case. Specific terms of the settlement agreement were not disclosed. This cash inflow will further improve its liquidity. According to the latest data disclosed by Hyperscale Data, the company's Bitcoin holdings have increased to 633.8609 Bitcoins, including 586.6674 Bitcoins held by its wholly-owned subsidiary Sentinum (approximately 440.2341 Bitcoins acquired on the open market and approximately 146.4333 Bitcoins obtained from its Bitcoin mining operations), and approximately 47.1935 Bitcoins purchased from the open market by another subsidiary, ACG. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Hyperscale Data Receives $26.6 Million Settlement, Enhances Liquidity
data.description:
Hyperscale Data, a publicly traded Bitcoin treasury company, has announced that its subsidiaries, Ault Lending LLC and RiskOn International, have received a settlement payment totaling approximately $26.6 million from a lawsuit that lasted several years. According to Odaily, the specific terms of the settlement agreement have not been disclosed, but the influx of cash is expected to improve the company's liquidity.

Hyperscale Data also revealed updated figures regarding its Bitcoin holdings, which have increased to 633.8609 BTC. This includes 586.6674 BTC held by its wholly-owned subsidiary, Sentinum, acquired through the purchase of approximately 440.2341 BTC on the open market and 146.4333 BTC obtained through its Bitcoin mining operations. Additionally, another subsidiary, ACG, has acquired approximately 47.1935 BTC from the open market.

data.title: Court Sentences Defendant in Virtual Currency Laundering Case
data.description: A court in Tianshui has sentenced a defendant to two years and four months in prison for involvement in a virtual currency laundering case. According to NS3.AI, the case involved laundering more than 390,000 yuan. The court revealed that the defendant converted the cash into virtual currency, earning 21,500 yuan from the activity.

data.title: CWFC Project Concludes Subscription with High Participation
data.description:
The second phase of the CWFC project concluded its subscription on April 4 at 16:00, with the Token Generation Event (TGE) set to be completed by 20:00 the same day. According to ChainCatcher, the event attracted over 42,000 participants, with a total subscription amount of 58,563,157 USDT, exceeding the target by nearly 59 times. Following asset settlement, there will be no lock-up period, allowing users to immediately sell or withdraw their holdings.

The project continued to offer a multi-currency participation mechanism, enabling users to subscribe using JU, USDT, BTC, or ETH, with allocations standardized in USDT terms. A dynamic winning rate model was introduced, increasing the rate by 0.2% for every additional 5,000,000 USDT raised. Participants who successfully invited friends to join could share up to 20% of the final token issuance as a reward.

data.title: Tianshui court tried a case involving the laundering of virtual currency proceeds: the defendant was sentenced to two years and four months in prison for "running errands to withdraw cash".
data.description: The People's Court of Qinzhou District, Tianshui City, Gansu Province, recently tried a criminal case involving the withdrawal of cash packages through virtual currency. The defendant, knowing the money was obtained through fraud, repeatedly withdrew a total of over 390,000 yuan in cash over seven days in areas without surveillance coverage. He then converted the funds into virtual currency and transferred it to his superior, profiting 21,500 yuan in the process. The court found him guilty of concealing and covering up the proceeds of crime and sentenced him to two years and four months in prison, a fine of 10,000 yuan, and ordered him to return the illegal gains. The judge warned that offline cash withdrawals have become a key step in laundering stolen funds from telecommunications fraud. "Cash withdrawal services" and "high-paying daily settlements" are often part of fraud chains. The public should be vigilant about receiving cash, transferring money, and assisting in the transfer of funds to avoid becoming accomplices to crime. (Tianshui Qinzhou District People's Court Official WeChat Account)

data.title: Bitcoin Derivatives Market Shows High Protective Demand, Suggesting Bullish Signal
data.description:
VanEck's Head of Research, Matthew Sigel, recently shared insights on the X platform regarding the current state of the Bitcoin derivatives market. According to Odaily, Sigel noted that the demand for protective measures in this market has reached the 99th percentile, typically seen as a 'contrarian buy signal' during periods of extreme risk aversion. He suggests that the present market conditions are favorable for establishing long positions.

Sigel also manages the VanEck Digital Transformation ETF (NODE), which has risen by 27% since its inception, while Bitcoin has declined by 33% in the same period. The ETF's strategy of diversified allocation and focus on profitable sectors has resulted in lower volatility. However, Sigel cautions that substantial capital expenditures in the artificial intelligence (AI) sector may exert significant pressure on the market if they fail to yield expected returns, particularly given the concentration of weight in S&P 500 constituent stocks.

data.title: U.S. and Israel Strike Iranian Petrochemical Zone, Casualties Likely
data.description: The United States and Israel have conducted a strike on the Mahshahr Petrochemical Special Economic Zone in Iran. According to NS3.AI, the Iranian news agency ISNA reported that casualties are highly likely as a result of this attack.

data.title: Federal Regulators Propose Capital Changes for Major Wall Street Banks
data.description: Federal regulators have put forward a proposal to adjust capital requirements, potentially reducing the necessary capital for the largest Wall Street banks by nearly 5%. According to NS3.AI, the Federal Reserve estimates that this package could free up approximately $20 billion for the eight largest banks. However, the proposal maintains that certain large regional banks must account for unrealized losses in their regulatory capital, a measure linked to the collapse of Silicon Valley Bank in 2023.

data.title: Opinion: Protective demand in the Bitcoin derivatives market has reached historical extremes, signaling a contrarian bullish stance.
data.description: Matthew Sigel, Head of Research at VanEck, analyzed on the X platform that the current protective demand in the Bitcoin derivatives market has risen to the 99th percentile historically, which is generally seen as a "contrarian long signal" in an extremely risk-averse market, and he judged that the market is currently suitable for establishing long positions. Sigel also manages the VanEck Digital Transformation ETF (NODE), which has risen 27% since its inception, while Bitcoin has fallen 33% over the same period, achieving lower volatility through diversified allocation and focusing on profitable sectors. However, he also warned that the huge capital expenditures by companies in the field of artificial intelligence (AI) could put substantial pressure on the market if they do not generate corresponding returns, especially given the heavy weighting of S&P 500 constituent stocks. Note: Percentile is a statistical position concept; the 99th percentile is a relatively extreme level, and the 50th percentile represents the median.

data.title: Tether may postpone its fundraising if its $500 billion valuation financing encounters a cold reception.
data.description: Tether is pushing investors to participate in a new funding round at a valuation of approximately $500 billion, but the fundraising process may be delayed if market demand falls short of expectations. The report states that the company has been exploring funding plans since last year, but some investors are cautious about the valuation. If this valuation is achieved, Tether will be larger than most US banks, second only to JPMorgan Chase. Previously, the company discussed raising approximately $15 billion to $20 billion through a private placement, but those plans remain uncertain. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Hyperliquid Whale Positions Reach $3.458 Billion, Data Shows
data.description:
Hyperliquid platform whales currently hold positions totaling $3.458 billion, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions account for $1.766 billion, representing 51.07% of the total, while short positions amount to $1.692 billion, making up 48.93%. Long positions have incurred a loss of $119 million, whereas short positions have gained $124 million.

A notable whale address, 0xa5b0..41, has taken a 15x leveraged long position on ETH at a price of $2,148.7, resulting in an unrealized loss of $6.7624 million.

data.title: IMF Urges Japan to Continue Rate Hikes Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description:
On April 4, Jin10 reported that despite the Middle East conflict posing a significant new risk to Japan's economic outlook, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) has urged the Bank of Japan to continue raising interest rates. According to Jin10, the IMF's recommendation comes as the market widely anticipates a potential rate hike by the Bank of Japan as early as April. The conflict-driven rise in oil prices and the weakening yen have increased import costs, exacerbating inflationary pressures in Japan.

The IMF stated that although economic growth is expected to slow, partly due to the war in Iran, moderate wage growth will support consumer spending. The risks to Japan's economic outlook and inflation are broadly balanced, with inflation projected to return to the Bank of Japan's 2% target level by 2027. As core inflation gradually aligns with the central bank's target, the IMF suggests that Japan should continue gradual rate hikes in a flexible, well-communicated, and data-dependent manner, moving towards a neutral interest rate level.

Furthermore, the IMF emphasized the importance of maintaining a flexible exchange rate mechanism, as it serves as a reliable buffer against external shocks.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase to Allocate $80 Billion for Small Business Financing in Underserved U.S. Communities
data.description: JPMorgan Chase has announced its American Dream initiative, which aims to provide $80 billion in financing to small businesses across the United States. According to NS3.AI, the program is designed to support underserved communities, with a particular focus on minority-owned and low-to-moderate income businesses. This initiative is part of the bank's broader effort to promote economic growth and inclusivity in areas that have historically faced financial challenges.

data.title: Nevada court extends injunction against Kalshi sports betting market
data.description: A Nevada judge ruled that Kalshi's sports betting prediction market products are "materially indistinguishable" from betting activities, extending the previously issued temporary injunction and granting a preliminary injunction filed by the state's betting regulator, prohibiting Kalshi from continuing to offer related contract services in the state until further proceedings. The injunction, initially issued on March 20, covers sports, entertainment, and election-related prediction markets. The regulator believes the products should be governed by state betting laws, while Kalshi argues that they fall under federally regulated derivatives markets. The question of regulatory jurisdiction remains unresolved. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Middle East Conflict Threatens Global Supply Chains Amid Oil Shortage
data.description:
The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has entered its second month, posing a significant risk of exacerbating the global oil shortage crisis. According to BlockBeats, the disruption has severely restricted the transportation of oil and natural gas through the Strait of Hormuz, reducing global supply by approximately one-fifth.

This interruption has not only driven up fuel prices but also strained the supply of petrochemical products essential for manufacturing everyday items such as shoes, clothing, and plastic bags. As the prices of materials like plastic, rubber, and polyester rise, the pressure is spreading across consumer markets.

Asia, home to over half of the world's manufacturing and heavily reliant on imported oil and commodities, is experiencing the most significant impact. Dan Martin, co-head of business intelligence at Deloitte Xianchi Advisory, noted that the effects will quickly extend to all goods, including beer, noodles, chips, toys, and cosmetics, due to the increasing difficulty in sourcing plastic bottle caps, transport pallets, snack packaging bags, and containers.

Martin further explained that adhesives used in footwear and furniture, industrial lubricants for machinery, paints, and solvents required for cleaning processes are also dependent on petroleum-derived products.

data.title: Bitcoin Transaction Demonstrated Without Internet at BOSS Summit
data.description:
On April 4, a groundbreaking demonstration took place at the BOSS Summit in Dharamsala, India, showcasing a Bitcoin transaction conducted entirely without internet access. According to BlockBeats, developer Bala executed the transaction using Mesh Radio, a wireless mesh network based on Meshtastic LoRa technology, bypassing traditional internet service providers, Wi-Fi, and cellular data.

The transaction was broadcast in real-time via radio waves directly into the Bitcoin mempool, completing the process from 'Off-Grid' to 'On-Chain.' This demonstration highlighted Bitcoin's resilience as an unseizable currency, particularly useful in scenarios involving censorship, internet outages, disasters, or uncertain times. Bitshala officials emphasized that this is what they mean by building an unseizable currency for uncertain times.

The relevant open-source repository has been made available, supporting the connection between Meshtastic devices and Bitcoin Core. It allows transaction data to be split into small packets, transmitted hop-by-hop across mesh nodes, and ultimately broadcast by an exit node to the global Bitcoin network.

The demonstration has sparked significant discussion within the community, being seen as a landmark advancement in the integration of mesh radio technology with Bitcoin. It proves that the Bitcoin network can maintain basic functionality even when traditional communication infrastructure fails.

data.title: Nevada Judge Extends Ban on Kalshi's Sports Contracts
data.description: A Nevada judge has extended a temporary ban on Kalshi's sports-related contracts, indicating plans to grant a preliminary injunction requested by the Nevada Gaming Control Board. According to NS3.AI, the judge prolonged the restraining order initially issued on March 20 by an additional two weeks to finalize the language of the injunction. The original order also prohibited entertainment and election-related bets.
